question: Looking at the US median income map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The median income gap between South Dakota and Missouri might exceed that between Minnesota and Iowa.
options.B: South Dakota and Missouri have roughly similar median incomes.
options.C: Minnesota and Iowa have almost identical median incomes.
options.D: The median income differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: South Dakota (65.1$k) and Missouri (106.3$k) have a 41.2$k median income difference, while Minnesota (59.6$k) and Iowa (62.7$k) have only a 3.1$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups South Dakota and Missouri into the same color bin (60$k+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Minnesota and Iowa appear more different due to different color bins.
